suppress.notes: file name: webpack-dev-server:4.15.2 Also suppressed in public/app/themes/clarity/osv-scanner.toml. The vulnerability only applies to developers running webpack-dev-server locally and gives access only to code that, in your case, is already open source. Therefore, it poses no meaningful security risk to your project.

file name: webpack-dev-server:4.15.2 Also suppressed in public/app/themes/clarity/osv-scanner.toml. The vulnerability only impacts developers running webpack-dev-server locally with a non-Chromium browser, and the only asset at risk is source code that is already publicly available. It poses no meaningful security risk.

file name: elliptic:6.6.1 Also suppressed in public/app/themes/clarity/osv-scanner.toml. The vulnerability affects applications that actively use elliptic for cryptographic signing. Laravel-mix does not, so the issue poses no practical security risk to your production application.
